content: Orphan drug designation criteria: disease prevalence <200,000 US patients OR >200,000 patients with inability to recover development costs from US sales. Prevalence calculation uses epidemiological data, considering disease definition, diagnostic criteria, and population estimates. Controversies include: 'salami slicing' (narrowly defining diseases to qualify), blockbuster orphan drugs (some generate >$1B annually despite small populations), high prices (median annual cost >$100,000), and potential abuse where companies initially seek orphan designation then expand to broader indications. Recent reforms address some concerns while maintaining core incentives. Approximately one-third of novel drug approvals now have orphan designation, raising questions about whether the program has exceeded its original intent.
